mechanism_narrative: When the Federal Funds Rate exceeds a restrictive threshold, higher borrowing costs for corporations and consumers compress economic activity and future earnings expectations, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over the medium term.
evidence_refs: fact:The FOMC has raised rates consistently through 2005, reaching 4.0% in November.
failure_condition: S&P 500 60-day forward returns are positive in more than 50% of instances when the trigger condition is met.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained positive growth in nonfarm payrolls is a direct indicator of a robust economy, which supports higher corporate revenues and profits, thus driving positive equity market returns.
failure_condition: The 3-month rolling change in PAYEMS exhibits a negative or statistically insignificant (p > 0.05) correlation with 20-day forward S&P 500 returns over a 5-year period.

mechanism_narrative: An overheated housing market, indicated by very high housing starts, combined with stagnating retail sales suggests that housing wealth effects are not translating into broad consumption, signaling consumer stress and a future downturn in equities.
evidence_refs: fact:Housing starts have been at historically high levels in 2005.
failure_condition: The joint trigger condition is not followed by negative 60-day S&P 500 returns in at least 60% of occurrences during a walk-forward test.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating consumer price inflation erodes real corporate profit margins and forces the central bank to maintain a restrictive policy stance. This combination of margin pressure and high discount rates leads to negative equity returns.
failure_condition: The correlation between CPIAUCSL changes and S&P 500 returns is not significantly more negative when FEDFUNDS is above 3.5% compared to when it is below.

mechanism_narrative: A positive surprise or acceleration in industrial production reflects broad strength in the manufacturing and production sectors, signaling robust near-term corporate earnings which drives positive equity returns over short horizons.
failure_condition: The 1-month change in INDPRO fails to show a statistically significant positive rank correlation with 5-day forward S&P 500 returns over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate falls to a very low level, it signals a tight labor market that will generate wage-push inflation. The anticipation of rising labor costs compressing corporate margins leads to negative forward equity returns.
evidence_refs: fact:The unemployment rate for November 2005 was 5.0%.
failure_condition: Mean 60-day S&P 500 returns are not statistically lower (t-test p > 0.05) in periods when UNRATE is below 5.0% compared to periods when it is above 5.0%.

mechanism_narrative: The year-over-year growth rate of real GDP is the most comprehensive measure of economic health. A strong growth rate signals a positive environment for aggregate corporate earnings, supporting higher equity prices over the next quarter.
failure_condition: The rolling 5-year correlation between year-over-year GDPC1 growth and 60-day forward S&P 500 returns falls below 0.1 and stays there for more than one year.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid increase in the unemployment rate signals a sharp deterioration in the labor market and the onset of a recession. This shock to household income and confidence causes a sharp decline in equity markets.
failure_condition: In a walk-forward test, more than 40% of triggered instances are followed by positive 20-day S&P 500 returns.
